title: viscum album subsp. austriacum
intro: Viscum album subsp. austriacum, also known as European mistletoe, is a semi-parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various tree species. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like viscotoxins and alkaloids. The health benefits of Viscum album subsp. austriacum include its potential to stimulate the immune system, reduce tumor growth, and alleviate symptoms of certain autoimmune diseases. Viscum album subsp. austriacum can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including dried extracts, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully considered under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
